scenario.scale: IT operations for smaller departments and mobile workplaces
scenario.context: Backup runs look technically green, but the backup strategy is not yet clearly described. In particular, full, incremental, differential and the generation principle should be clear.
scenario.mission: Map data protection, backup strategies, generation principle, offsite storage and restore testing into a clear topic structure.

source.note: The functional coordination expects a clear separation between backup type, retention, recovery and evidence.

station.profile: Backup strategies, generation principle and restore evidence
constraints: Backup strategy and restore evidence should be clearly separated but connected
Generation principle and retention period must be visible
A green job status does not replace a restore test

customerRequest.subject: Prepare backup strategy and recovery in a traceable way
customerRequest.body:
Hello,

we need a compact but reliable presentation of the backup strategy. Please separate full, incremental, differential, GFS and restore testing so the department can directly follow the logic later.

Best regards
Operations

contentBlocks.text: Backups run regularly, but the strategy has not been clearly named yet. The team now needs to place the terms for backup type, generations, retention, restore test and evidence so a viable plan emerges.

questions.title: Distinguish backup types
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes full, incremental and differential backups?

options.text: A full backup captures a complete state; incremental backs up changes since the last backup; differential backs up changes since the last full backup.
options.rationale: This is the technically correct distinction.

options.text: Incremental and differential mean the same thing, just with a different name.
Differential backs up only new folders, incremental only old folders.
A full backup is only a log without actual data.

feedback.correct: The backup types were correctly distinguished.
feedback.wrong: Pay attention to the relation to the full backup and to changes since the last backup.

questions.title: Recognize a reliable backup strategy
questions.prompt: Which points belong to a reliable backup strategy? Multiple statements can be correct.

options.text: Full backups and complementary backup types are planned.
options.rationale: The backup type must match the protection needs.
Without a retention period the strategy is incomplete.

options.text: Separate storage or offsite backups are planned.
options.rationale: This keeps a copy safe even if local issues occur.

options.text: A restore test is explicitly planned.
options.rationale: Only a tested restore makes the backup reliable.

options.text: Backup logs are kept for evidence.
options.rationale: Without logs there is no audit trail.

options.text: A green job status is sufficient evidence.
options.rationale: Job status does not replace a recovery test.

options.text: Backups need no documentation as long as there is free storage.
options.rationale: Storage alone says nothing about recoverability.

feedback.correct: The core elements of the backup strategy were correctly recognized.
feedback.wrong: Think about type, retention, storage, test and logs instead of just job status.

questions.title: Order backup and restore flow
questions.prompt: Which sequence makes sense for backup and recovery?
items: Define data scope and backup target.
Run full and follow-up backups according to plan.
Run a restore test on a test system.
correctOrder: Define data scope and backup target.
Run full and follow-up backups according to plan.
Run a restore test on a test system.

feedback.correct: The sequence for backup and recovery is correct.
feedback.wrong: First plan, then back up, then test and document.

questions.title: Assign backup terms
questions.prompt: Which description best fits each term?

pairs.right: Complete backup of an approved data state.
Only changes since the last backup are stored.
Only changes since the last full backup are stored.
Generation rotation with three levels for backups.
Backup is stored separately from the primary location.
Time span for how long backups are kept.
Planned time window in which backups are executed.

options: Complete backup of an approved data state.
Only changes since the last backup are stored.
Only changes since the last full backup are stored.
Generation rotation with three levels for backups.
Backup is stored separately from the primary location.
Time span for how long backups are kept.
Planned time window in which backups are executed.

feedback.correct: The backup terms were assigned consistently.
feedback.wrong: Check backup type, rotation, storage, retention and recovery testing.
